## Load in necessary libraries

```{r, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
```

## Importing Democracy Data from Github

```{r, message=FALSE}
democracy_data <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2024/2024-11-05/democracy_data.csv')
```

## Describing the context of the data
This data has information on regimes of countries from 1950 to 2022. This includes what type of government the country has, who the ruler is, if they have fair and free elections, and tons of more information.

## Data Cleaning
There has been a fair amount of data cleaning done to the set. This includes renaming the variables, turning placeholder data (e.g. ?) into missing values, and splitting a month/year field into two separate variables.

# Part 1

## Summary on: What countries have the most regime changes over time?

```{r}
count_regime_changes<-function(country){
  country_data<-democracy_data |> 
    filter(country_name==country)|> # Filters so data is only inputed country
    arrange(year) # Sorts data from earliest year to most recent year
  
regime<-country_data$regime_category_index

# Creates a vector that returns true when regime has been changed from previous year
changes<- regime != lag(regime) 

sum(changes, na.rm=TRUE) # Sum the amount of times the regime has changed

}

results<- democracy_data |>
  distinct(country_name) |> # Keeps only unique country names
  mutate(n_changes=map_dbl(
    country_name,count_regime_changes
    )) # Runs the function on each country and counts the number of changes
  
results |> slice_max(n_changes,n=10)

```

# Part 2

## Summary on: How often do countries have full suffrage when they are democratic?

```{r}
prop_suffrage_dem <- function(country) {
  democracy_data |>
    filter( # Keeps the years where the selected country is a democracy
      country_name == country,
      is_democracy == TRUE 
    ) |>
    # Computes the proportion of democratic years with full suffrage
    summarise(prop_suffrage = mean(has_full_suffrage, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    # Extracts value so function returns numeric result
    pull(prop_suffrage)
}
 
# Top 10 
democracy_data |>
  distinct(country_name) |>
  mutate(
    prop_suffrage = map_dbl(country_name, prop_suffrage_dem)
  ) |>
  slice_max(prop_suffrage, n = 10)

# Bottom 10
democracy_data |>
  distinct(country_name) |>
  mutate(
    prop_suffrage = map_dbl(country_name, prop_suffrage_dem)
  ) |>
  slice_min(prop_suffrage, n = 10)
  
```

Each number represents the proportion of democractic years where the country had full suffrage.

# Part 3

## Summary on: **Proportion of country years (1950-2020) with a female leader within each regime, country, election system**

```{r}
sum_female_leader <- function(democ_data, columns, group){ 
    
    female_leader_any <- democ_data |> 
        rowwise() |>    # Selects the rows as groups to check over each year
        mutate(female_leader = any(c_across(all_of(columns)), na.rm = TRUE)) |>
                        # Checks across defined columns for a female leader
        ungroup()       # Undoes rowwise() grouping
    
    prop_female <- female_leader_any |> 
        group_by(across(all_of(group))) |>  # Grouped by group = argument
        summarise(prop_female_leader = mean(female_leader, na.rm = TRUE))
                        # mean of TRUE/FALSE for the proportion 

    return(prop_female) # returns the group argument and the proportion
}

sum_female_leader(democracy_data, 
                     columns = c("is_female_monarch", "is_female_president"), 
                     group = "country_name")

sum_female_leader(democracy_data, 
                     columns = c("is_female_monarch", "is_female_president"), 
                     group = "regime_category")

sum_female_leader(democracy_data, 
                     columns = c("is_female_monarch", "is_female_president"), 
                     group = "election_system")
```

Female_leader is a constructed variable for type of leader monarch and president. It is a logical for each row and either there is a female leader or not.

# Part 4

## Summary on: Which countries have the highest proportion of postponed elections over time?

```{r}
# postponed_elections creates a variable prop_postponed that reports the
# proportion of years with postponed elections for each country
postponed_elections <- function(country, data = democracy_data){
  result <- data |>
    filter(country_name == country) |>
    arrange(year) |>
    mutate(
      postponed = has_postponed_election == TRUE
    ) |>
    summarise(prop_postponed = mean(postponed, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(result$prop_postponed)
}

postponed_data <- democracy_data |>
  distinct(country_name) |>
  mutate(prop_postponed = map_dbl(country_name, postponed_elections)) |>
  arrange(desc(prop_postponed))

ggplot(postponed_data[1:10, ], 
       aes(x = reorder(country_name, -prop_postponed), y = prop_postponed)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "dodgerblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Countries with the Highest Proportion of Postponed Elections",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Proportion of Years with Postponed Elections"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
